name: Green Beans with Onion, Garlic, and Tomato
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the stems off the green beans, but leave the tails intact. Cut the beans into 1-inch pieces.
2. If desired, peel the tomatoes by dropping them into boiling water for 15 seconds, then removing to a bowl of ice water. Chop the tomatoes into half-inch pieces. Alternatively, use one large can of diced plum tomatoes.
3. Heat the olive oil in a non-stick pan and stir-fry the garlic for about 30 seconds.
4. Add the onions and continue stirring over medium heat until softened, being careful not to burn the garlic or color the onions, for 2-3 minutes.
5. Add the chopped tomatoes, beans, salt, pepper, oregano (if using), and water. Bring the mixture to a boil.
6. Reduce the heat and simmer covered for 10-15 minutes, depending on desired tenderness of the beans.
7. Uncover the pan and increase the heat to reduce the liquid until thickened.
8. Adjust seasonings as needed. Serve over rice for a healthy and satisfying meal.
